posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked: Sami-Odi Shiraz, Little Wine #9, Barossa Valley. I’ve been wanting to try a Sami-Odi Wine for a while now but they are…‘difficult’ to source here in the States/AZ. I was finally able to snag a few for (relatively) decent prices and was excited to open one. Even with really high expectations, I am happy to report that the wine more than exceeded them. A big wine for sure but you’d never know it. It first hits your palate with an explosion of black fruits, cinnamon, and olive to the point where you fully expect it to be a huge, heavy wine…but somehow it hits your mid-palate with a rush of acidity and everything feels incredibly balanced, and almost medium bodied. Each sip feels like an adventure and you literally can’t wait to take the next one. A truly exciting wine that I wish I had a bunch of in the cellar. The hype is real.

posts.cooked: Not sure what the wait list is like, and yes, shipping cost from AUS is pricey (around $275/case, so it adds $20-25 to each bottle), but the all-in bottle price still comes out to a better price than I’m seeing at retail.

posts.name: Demian Saenz
posts.cooked: I tried to get on the list and I was willing to order a case to ship to the US, but Fraser said he doesn’t ship to the US. This was in 2019 after I brought back 2 bottles from a trip to AU, so I’m lucky to have tried it, and still have 1 bottle remaining.

posts.name: Cameron Clark
posts.cooked:
I believe there is a waiting list now to get on the mailing list - same with Wendouree.

If you look at Fraser’s site, so can see that the photos are in place for the next vintage - I would expect a mailer to be sent in the early new year

I joined when I was living in the Netherlands and it was due to this board I believe (a William Kelly review, although maybe it was the Squires board then (2014)). Shipping was a lot, but I am glad I joined. There are now 3 wines produced and the production looks to be ‘gigantic’ for the next vintage - seems to be about 1100+ cases, so maybe more people will get to experience the love.

If you like the style then there are a couple of new kids on the block. David Powell’s son Callum is making a name for himself with Agricola and a friend of his has released her own wine under the Setiono label. Both release a wine from the Flaxman Valley and their ‘21s are great.

Shipping & customs takes 2 to 4 weeks, so end of April at my door. I live in Massachusetts so it’s still mild temps in April, even chances of snow, or do you want fewer than 6 bottles?

posts.name: Dave English
posts.cooked:
Offer came out yesterday, looks like some great wines.

" 2021 Sami-Odi ‘Hoffmann Dallwitz’ (Syrah)

Our ‘Hors Choix’ or ‘Super Choice’ derived from the Hoffmann’s two oldest plots, sown in 1927 (ER-27) and between 1888-1912 (DW-OLD).
These 22 Burgundian Pièces were carefully assembled and bottled amid the spring of 2022 after enduring a 78 week elevage. Bottles were filled without filtration, fining or sparging (the removal of naturally occurring oxygen & C02) and all transfer’s were completed via gravity prior to bottling.

This can be enjoyed now or cellared for as long as your taste desires. It adores air and will continue to unfurl over many days if kept in cool conditions.

Yields ranged between 26 & 39 hectolitres per hectare.
6275 bottles were filled.

Sami-Odi ‘Little Wine’ #12 (Syrah)

An unvintaged assemblage composed of casks from 2022 (49%), 2021 (23%), 2020 (11%), 2019 (8%), 2018 (1%), 2017 (4%), 2016 (2%) & 2015 (2%). Sourced from our six treasured plots within the Hoffmann’s ‘Dallwitz’ vineyard from vines planted in 1996, 1995, 1994, 1960, 1927 & the oldest vines planted prior to 1912.

Enjoy now whilst vibrant or cellar with moderation.
6430 bottles were filled.

2021 Sami-Odi ‘Our Hill’ (Syrah)

Our second harvest from our homes rolling hills on the South side of Angaston in the Eden Valley.

Lithe and delicate, ‘OH’ is best enjoyed over the next few years and contrary to our Hoffmann wines is at its finest on the day of opening.

Yields ranged between 16 & 41 hectolitres per hectare.

1376 bottles were filled.

posts.name: Dave English
posts.cooked: The typical Sami-Odi intensity, but not quite dialled up to 11. Texturally I am loving the silky mouthfeel without it being cloying. Huge bouquet. I grabbed a 3 pack of this years vintage so will be looking forward to them while I wait for the flagship ones to get a bit more age on them.
